lastReleaseMessage: v0.10.0: bar-raise drain (D-006 loopback bind, D-007 import/rollback strict backup-fail, error_log + logEvent, daily prune, regex hoist, analytics surface modules, dedup guard, dashboard preservation, drift-fix F1-F8); .finances/ removed from git index (D-001/D-002 enforcement)

barRaise.topFinding: The eight-cycle stall finally broke at the cause, not the symptom: today's canonical audit traced 'nothing ever commits' to a zero-byte, two-day-stale .git/index.lock (2026-06-05) that let git status read the tree while silently failing every git add and commit, and cleared it (verified gone this run, write path healthy). In the same window a real v0.11.0 frontend polish pass landed in the tree (Badge/Spinner/Tooltip/FilterChip/PageHeader/SectionAnchor, a hooks/ dir, format helpers, CSS, POLISH_LOG.md), so the uncommitted surface is the largest yet and for the first time holds un-recreatable source. HEAD is still e6cac08 (2026-05-29, nine days cold), nothing has committed, and three smaller drifts rode in: POLISH_LOG.md asserts a shipped v0.11.0 no version marker agrees with, six files are orphaned (verified zero importers), and there is still no .gitattributes. The verdict moves two tiers off stalled because the blocker is genuinely cleared and real work is in flight, but it is contingent: if the next cycle still shows e6cac08, it regresses.
